questions.question: Under DPDPA 2023, when must notice be given to the Data Principal?
options: Before or at the time of seeking consent

questions.question: A notice under DPDPA must be available in:
options: English or any of the languages in the Eighth Schedule

questions.question: If a Data Fiduciary previously processed data without notice, it must:
options: Issue a fresh notice as soon as practicable

questions.question: DPDPA consent must be:
options: Free, specific, informed, unconditional, unambiguous and clear affirmative

questions.question: A Data Principal's right to withdraw consent shall be:
Consent of a child requires:
options: Verifiable consent of parent or lawful guardian

questions.question: Personal data may be used only for:
options: The specified purpose for which consent was given

questions.question: Repurposing data for marketing without fresh consent is:
Section 7 'legitimate uses' include:
options: Specified categories such as employment, medical emergency, court orders

questions.question: Data minimisation means:
options: Collect only what is necessary for the specified purpose

questions.question: An app demanding contacts to send a payment is likely:
When designing a form, minimisation requires:
options: Justifying every field against the stated purpose

questions.question: Data Fiduciaries must ensure data is:
options: Accurate, complete and up-to-date when used for decisions

questions.question: If a Data Principal requests correction of inaccurate data, the Fiduciary must:
options: Correct, complete or update without undue delay

questions.question: Accuracy obligation is heightened when data is used for:
Storage limitation requires data to be erased when:
options: The specified purpose is served and retention is no longer necessary or required by law

questions.question: A Fiduciary must also cause its Data Processors to:
options: Erase the data on cessation of purpose

questions.question: Retention beyond purpose may be permitted only when:
options: Required by any law in force

questions.question: DPDPA requires reasonable security safeguards to prevent:
On a personal data breach, the DF must notify:
options: The Board and affected Data Principals

questions.question: Reasonable safeguards include:
options: Technical and organisational measures appropriate to the risk

questions.question: The Data Fiduciary is accountable for:
options: Compliance with the Act including by its Processors

questions.question: Significant Data Fiduciaries must additionally:
options: Appoint a DPO based in India and conduct DPIA & periodic audits

questions.question: Penalties under the Act may extend up to:
options: ₹250 crore per instance for certain breaches

questions.question: Data Principals have the right to:
options: Access, correction, erasure and grievance redressal

questions.question: Grievance redressal under DPDPA must be:
options: Provided as a readily available means by the DF

questions.question: Right to nominate allows a Data Principal to:
options: Nominate another individual to exercise rights in event of death/incapacity
